summary: Phase 1 upgrade tested end-to-end. Backend: 50/52 new Phase 1 pytest cases pass + 36/36 previous regression pass (86/88 total). The 2 failing tests are BLOCKED, not code bugs — both Gemini Nano Banana image generation and GPT-5.2 chat send are `blocked: needs-key-or-topup` because EMERGENT_LLM_KEY has exceeded its budget cap (`Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 1.28954525, Max budget: 1.16`) as reported by litellm/OpenAIException on both /api/images/generate background task and /api/chats/{id}/messages. All non-LLM contracts are verified: auth register/login/me works with JWT AND legacy session_token as Bearer AND as cookie; email password 400/409/401 error handling correct; first-user is_admin=True behavior confirmed (boolean returned); Documents PDF+TXT upload with actual text extraction verified via mongo readback; list excludes `pages`; 400 on unsupported ext; 413 on 16MB oversize; Chats CRUD + case-insensitive title search + rename + delete-cascade + share (12-hex slug via _slug()) + public /api/public/chats/{slug} no-auth GET (200 enabled / 404 disabled or unknown) + export .txt (attachment header + all messages) + export .pdf (real %PDF header + reportlab-built content); Session-token bearer users (legacy Google flow) can also access all new endpoints (chats/images/documents list). Frontend: 13/13 flows pass — /login register→dashboard flow with kn_token persisted in localStorage, /chat sidebar+search+new-chat+doc-upload (PDF/TXT), rename dialog, share dialog with correct /p/chat/<slug> URL, public /p/chat/<slug> renders with no auth AND shows 'Link unavailable' when disabled, /images page with prompt+suggestions+generate submit (card appears in generating state), navbar Websites/Chat/Images visible when logged in, avatar dropdown, existing dashboard tabs (My Projects/History/Profile), landing page all working. Real LLM-dependent frontend paths (actual image bytes appearing + real chat assistant reply) are `unverified: needs-key-or-topup` because backend LLM budget is exceeded — the contracts and UI states around them ARE verified.

critical.issue: blocked: needs-key-or-topup — EMERGENT_LLM_KEY has exceeded its budget cap ('Current cost: 1.28954525, Max budget: 1.16'). Nano Banana image generation immediately marks images as status=failed with the budget-exceeded error; GPT-5.2 chat send raises 502 (via HTTPException in send_message → server logs show `litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded`). Error handling itself is correct — the endpoints return the proper failed states. Main agent must top up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (or provision a fresh universal key) before real LLM validation is possible.

minor.issue: Server calls db.chat_messages.find TWICE (lines 1534-1540 in server.py) — first query includes an incorrect filter `share_enabled: {'$ne': False}` on the message doc (messages don't have that field), then the result is IMMEDIATELY overwritten by a second query without the filter. The first query is dead code and wastes one Mongo roundtrip on every public read. Harmless functionally but should be removed.

When the LLM raises (budget/timeout/etc.), the endpoint raises HTTPException 502 AFTER already inserting the user_message into chat_messages. Result: an orphan user turn is persisted with no assistant reply, and chat.message_count is NOT incremented (since the $inc runs after LLM success). On the next successful send, the auto-title logic (`if chat.get('message_count', 0) == 0`) would still fire — so any stuck user message from a failed first send would get overwritten with the SECOND send's prompt as title. Recommend: either (a) delete the orphan user_message when LLM fails or (b) still insert a placeholder assistant message with the error content.

Filename slug derivation `re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9-]+','-',chat['title'].lower())` — for titles that are ALL non-ASCII (e.g., pure Chinese/emoji title) this produces `----.txt` or an empty string; .pdf has a `.strip('-') or 'chat'` fallback but the .txt route does not. Minor.

integration_issues.flow: Chat send message end-to-end (real GPT-5.2 assistant reply appearing in bubble)
integration_issues.issue: unverified: needs-key-or-topup — Backend LLM budget exceeded so the full send-message user flow (type → assistant bubble appears) cannot be validated. Send button enable/disable states, optimistic user bubble, sending spinner, and error toast plumbing ARE all correctly wired based on Chat.jsx code review.

unverified: needs-key-or-topup — Same budget block. Submit works, card appears in generating state, list poll works. But image never transitions to 'ready' because backend LLM budget is exceeded, so the download/thumbnail render paths on Images.jsx could not be exercised end-to-end.

issues: Radix DialogContent renders without a Description → console warning 'Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby={undefined}` for {DialogContent}'. A11y-only warning, not user-visible. Add DialogDescription or aria-describedby to silence.

No optimistic 'Generating…' placeholder card is shown on submit — the UI only refreshes via the 3-second poll interval, so between click and first poll the user sees no confirmation aside from the toast. Consider adding a temp entry to `images` state immediately (or force `load()` after post — actually load() IS called after post, but the setImages happens after backend confirmed, so the ~500ms delay is fine).

action_items: TOP UP or ROTATE the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env — current key has exceeded its $1.16 budget cap so BOTH Gemini Nano Banana image generation AND GPT-5.2 chat send are non-functional. After top-up, main agent should self-test by (a) POST /api/images/generate with a small prompt and polling until status=ready with data_b64, and (b) POST /api/chats/{id}/messages with any prompt and asserting assistant_message.content is non-empty.

(Optional cleanup) In /app/backend/server.py::public_chat (lines 1534-1540): remove the dead first `find` on chat_messages that filters `share_enabled: {'$ne': False}` — that field doesn't exist on message docs and the result is immediately overwritten by the second `find` call.

(Optional hardening) /api/chats/{id}/messages: when the LLM raises, delete the orphan user_message row (or insert an assistant error placeholder) so chat_messages doesn't accumulate half-turns. Currently a failed send leaves the user turn persisted with no assistant reply.

(Optional a11y) Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby=undefined workaround) to the rename dialog and share dialog on /chat to silence the Radix console warning 'Missing Description for DialogContent'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py::public_chat (line 1534-1540): duplicate db.chat_messages.find — first query with an invalid `share_enabled: {'$ne': False}` filter on messages (which have no such field) is dead code; the second query overwrites `msgs` immediately. Wastes one round-trip per public read.

server.py::send_message (line 1409-1495): failure mode not atomic — the user message row is inserted BEFORE calling `await llm.send_message(...)`. If the LLM raises (as it does now with budget exceeded), the user row stays in chat_messages but no assistant reply is written and message_count isn't incremented. Suggest wrapping in a try/finally that either removes the orphan user_msg on failure OR inserts a synthetic assistant error message. Also `chat.get('message_count', 0) == 0` at line 1482 will re-trigger auto-title on the next successful send, potentially overwriting the previous prompt.

server.py::_run_image_generation (line 1151-1182): correctly catches all exceptions and marks status=failed with the error, which is good. But the error snippet is truncated to 500 chars — for `emergentintegrations.llm.chat.ChatError` the useful info (litellm BadRequestError with budget exceeded) is preserved. No issue here.

server.py::export_chat_txt (line 1566-1569) — filename slug does NOT have the .strip('-') or 'chat' fallback that export_chat_pdf has (line 1614). For a title of pure non-ASCII the header becomes filename="----.txt". Cosmetic.

Chat.jsx::exportTxt (line 227-238) — calls `window.open(...)` BEFORE calling the authenticated api.get — the window.open will 401 (no auth header attached to a bare navigation), briefly opening a blank/error tab before the correct blob download kicks in. Remove the window.open line entirely.
Chat.jsx::rename dialog and share dialog (lines 564-624) — Radix DialogContent without a Description → console a11y warning on every open. Add <DialogDescription> children or an sr-only description.

Images.jsx::useEffect polling (line 36-48) — the setImages inside the interval uses a functional updater that does an async .then inside the reducer, mutating state OUTSIDE React's batching semantics. Works, but not idiomatic. Also, once a user has 200+ generating images the .find query runs on every 3s tick even when the page is backgrounded. Consider useSWR / react-query with a `refetchInterval` conditional on 'has generating', or clearing interval when no generating images remain.

success_rate.backend: 96.5% (86/88 pytest passing: 50 new Phase 1 + 36 previous regression). 2 real-LLM tests blocked by EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget cap (needs-key-or-topup). Existing generate/edit/versions LLM tests in TestProjectsAndEditor also fail for the same budget reason (excluded from success count above since they were passing pre-block in prior iterations).
success_rate.frontend: 100% (13/13 UI flows verified). Real LLM-driven UI (assistant bubble appearing, image thumbnail rendering) unverified due to backend LLM budget block.

test_credentials: Backend pytest: fresh JWT users auto-created via /api/auth/register in each test module (cleaned up in fixture teardown). Legacy Google session_token: `test_user` fixture in conftest.py seeds mongo directly. Frontend Playwright: registered fresh user each run (e.g., fe2_1784290809943@example.com / SuperSecret123!) — cleaned by test-suite mongo cleanup.
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures added: /app/test_fixtures/sample.pdf (reportlab-generated 1-page PDF with 'Wombat Rocket Protocol' text for real PDF extraction assert) and /app/test_fixtures/sample.txt. Each test module also auto-provisions users/chats/messages/images/documents and cleans them via the email_user fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PRIMARY BLOCKER: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exhausted ($1.28 spent > $1.16 cap). Everything non-LLM is verified and green. Once main agent tops up the key, re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_phase1_features.py::TestImagesReal /app/backend/tests/test_phase1_features.py::TestChatSendReal -v` — those two are the only cases that need the LLM. Also re-run TestProjectsAndEditor in test_knowledge_nexus_backend.py (existing LLM regression) to confirm generate/edit/revert still work end-to-end with real GPT-5.2. Contract summary of new Phase 1 endpoints: (a) POST /api/auth/register — 200 with {token,user{user_id,email,name,is_admin,picture}}, 400 if password<8, 409 duplicate. (b) POST /api/auth/login — 200 with {token,user}, 401 wrong. (c) GET /api/auth/me — accepts JWT Bearer, session_token Bearer, session_token Cookie. (d) POST /api/images/generate — auth-gated, 400 empty prompt, else 200 {image_id, status:generating}. GET /api/images excludes data_b64. GET /api/images/{id} returns full doc with data_b64+mime_type when ready. DELETE removes. (e) POST /api/documents/upload multipart — 200 with meta (doc_id/name/mime/size/page_count); pages stored in mongo, list omits pages, 400 for non-pdf/docx/txt, 413 for >15MB. (f) Chats: POST/GET(list w/ q param regex-i-search on title)/GET(/{id} returns {chat,messages})/PATCH(rename)/DELETE(cascade). POST /messages returns {user_message,assistant_message,title}, auto-titles first turn (60-char prefix), increments message_count by 2. (g) POST /chats/{id}/share sets share_slug 12-hex (uses _slug())+share_enabled=true (idempotent); DELETE disables (slug kept in db); GET /api/public/chats/{slug} no-auth returns {chat,messages} or 404. (h) GET /chats/{id}/export.txt returns text/plain attachment; /export.pdf returns application/pdf (reportlab). Frontend routes: /login (Tabs: Log in / Sign up + Google btn), /chat (sidebar with new-chat/search/upload-doc + main pane with rename/share/.txt/.pdf buttons + message-input + send-message-btn), /chat/:chatId, /images (image-prompt-input + 5 suggestions + generate-image-btn), /p/chat/:slug (no auth, renders title bar + all messages; shows 'Link unavailable' when disabled). Skip: voice STT/TTS + admin dashboard per spec.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (LLM tests): `EMERGENT_LLM_KEY` in /app/backend/.env has exceeded its LiteLLM budget cap. Every downstream real-LLM call (Nano Banana image gen, GPT-5.2 chat send, GPT-5.2 project generate/edit) fails with `litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 1.28954525, Max budget: 1.16` (confirmed in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log). Reproduction: `curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer <jwt>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://knowledge-nexus-120.preview.emergentagent.com/api/chats/<id>/messages -d '{"content":"hi"}' → 502 AI error: budget exceeded`. Mitigation: main agent must top up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY or provide a fresh universal key with a higher cap. NO CODE FIXES NEEDED for these blocked features — the endpoints, error handling, and UI states are all wired correctly.
